articles.title: Electricity bills to fall even further than predicted in state as renewables share nears 50 pct
articles.summary:
State regulator sets default price of power even lower than projected in March, giving the renewables-forward state government a win with voters ahead of the November state election. 
The post Electricity bills to fall even further than predicted in state as renewables share nears 50 pct appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Fortescue begins building 690 MW solar farm and 650 MWh battery system
articles.summary: One of Australia’s biggest miners has commenced construction of a 690 MW solar farm and 650 MWh battery energy storage system in Western Australia as part of plans to eliminate diesel and gas from its iron ore operations.

articles.title: Singapore team builds 19.7%-efficient ultrathin ‘biPoly’ TOPCon solar cell
articles.summary: The ultra-thin TOPCon solar cell uses dual-sided polysilicon passivated contacts formed by low-pressure chemical vapor deposition. The champion device, with a thickness of only 80 μm, achieved 19.7% efficiency with 719 mV open-circuit voltage and demonstrated strong passivation, optical performance, and mechanical flexibility.

articles.title: Record-dry April lifts irradiance across Eastern Australia
articles.summary: Solcast, a DNV company, reports that April 2026 was one of Australia’s driest Aprils in recent years, with widespread rainfall deficits driven by persistent high pressure and ENSO returning to neutral, leading to mostly clear skies and above-normal solar irradiance across much of the continent. New South Wales and Victoria saw the strongest sunshine surpluses, while remnants of ex-Tropical Cyclone Maila brought cloud and rain to the north, creating regional contrasts in rainfall and solar con...

articles.title: IRENA proposes global electrification target of 35% by 2035
articles.summary: New report from the International Renewable Energy Agency says renewables will be key to meeting rising global demand for electricity but with up to 2.5 TW of solar, storage and wind projects already in connection queues worldwide, increased investment in grid infrastructure will be pivotal.

articles.title: Fortescue starts construction of Australia’s biggest solar farm outside main grid, and giant eight-hour battery
articles.summary:
Fortescue starts building biggest solar farm outside main grid, and a giant eight-hour battery as it races to reach its target of real zero emissions by 2030.
The post Fortescue starts construction of Australia’s biggest solar farm outside main grid, and giant eight-hour battery appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Solar and battery hybrid projects prominent in latest CIS tender
articles.summary: The results of the latest tender under the federal government’s Capacity Investment Scheme have been released with 19 projects successful, aiming to provide a combined 7.8 GW of renewable generation capacity and 7.9 GWh of storage by the end of 2030.

articles.title: Australia’s biggest wind project and 8 battery hybrids among 19 winners of largest ever renewable auction
articles.summary:
Latest CIS tender dominated by wind projects and battery hybrids, with 7.8 GW of new renewable capacity. There are now 59 projects with CIS deals, how many will be built?
The post Australia’s biggest wind project and 8 battery hybrids among 19 winners of largest ever renewable auction appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.summary:

articles.title: CSIRO is cutting vital climate science jobs. Here’s what’s at stake for Australia
articles.summary:
Proposed cuts to staff in CSIRO's environment unit are likely to take in the team that runs the agency's foundational climate program. Here's what's at stake.
The post CSIRO is cutting vital climate science jobs. Here’s what’s at stake for Australia appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: “Not just a call centre:” Home battery contender doubles down on Australia-made strengths
articles.summary:
In the mad rush to home battery storage, this Australian company is doubling down its focus on a high quality, locally-based installation and service team.

articles.title: Sungrow says advanced inverter trials show they can provide heart-beat of the grid in absence of coal
articles.summary:
Chinese power company echoes findings of  Tesla and Fluence, saying tests show grid forming inverters can provide the heartbeat of the grid normally provided by coal.

articles.title: Australia just suffered its worst wind and solar “drought” in four years – but needed less gas backup
articles.summary:
What happens when the wind don't blow and the sun don't shine? Australia got a sneak peak on Monday in the worst wind and solar drought in four years.

articles.title: “No one can sanction the sun, no one can blockade the wind:” Bowen backs electrification at UN climate summit
articles.summary:
Australia and Turkey have revealed the global oil crisis will prompt a major focus on electrification at the United Nations climate talks in November.
The post “No one can sanction the sun, no one can blockade the wind:” Bowen backs electrification at UN climate summit appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Solar, heat pumps, electric cooktops: Another 8,000 social housing homes to get energy upgrades
articles.summary:
Federal Labor commits new investment to  fund state social housing upgrades like rooftop solar, electric heating and cooling systems and cooktops, hot water heat pumps and ceiling insulation.

articles.title: Data | Power: Navigating policy, regulation and networks
articles.summary: Australia is at a pivotal moment in its energy and digital transformation. The energy transition is driving electricity demand growth through electrification and unprecedented levels of investment in renewable generation.

articles.title: 22 solar projects share $1.9 million funding round for Solomon Islands communities
articles.summary: The Australian government has partnered with communities across the Solomon Islands to develop 22 solar powered streetlight and solar energy projects, through an inaugural program's $1.9 million funding round.

articles.title: Tasmania commits $1.8 billion for energy generation and asset upgrades
articles.summary: The 2026-27 Tasmania Budget and Forward Estimates has committed $1.8 billion for energy generation and network asset upgrades to future proof the state's electricity supply and Marinus Link connection.

articles.title: Spearmint Energy secures US$450 million for 600MWh Texas BESS project
articles.summary: Battery storage developer and operator Spearmint Energy has closed a US$450 million financing to support its 300MW/600MWh Red Egret battery energy storage system (BESS) project.

articles.title: LDES investor Frontier Power acquires 480MWh Texas BESS portfolio from Bimergen
articles.summary: Long-duration energy storage (LDES) development and investment company Frontier Power USA (FPUSA) has acquired a 480MWh battery energy storage system (BESS) portfolio from developer-operator Bimergen Energy.

articles.title: Germany market participants starting to price 2029-onwards projects despite uncertainty
articles.summary: The situation and uncertainty around grid connections and grid fees in Germany is evolving, possibly enabling market participants to look beyond the August 2029 grid fee exemption cut-off date - although uncertainty is still very high.

articles.title: EDF completes 120MWh Poland BESS; Eurus, NGEN and Aretis progress other CEE projects
articles.summary: EDF Power Solutions has commissioned a 50MW/120MWh BESS project in Poland, Eurus has done the same in Hungary, while NGEN and Aretis Group have financed and enlisted optimisers (respectively) for projects in Hungary, Romania, Poland, Slovenia and Latvia.

articles.title: Masdar picks Sungrow as supplier for world’s largest 24/7 renewable energy project in UAE
articles.summary: Masdar has selected Sungrow for the world’s first gigawatt-scale 24/7 dispatchable renewable energy project in the United Arab Emirates (UAE).

articles.title: UNSW researchers shed new light on UV-induced degradation in PERC, TOPCon solar cells
articles.summary: The scientists identified that UV exposure in PERC and TOPCon solar cells increases interface defects and makes them more recombination-active, driving ultraviolet-induced degradation. They found strong degradation on PERC front and rear surfaces and TOPCon front surfaces, while the TOPCon rear remains stable due to poly-Si UV absorption.

articles.title: 1KOMMA5° achieves 1 GW of shiftable load with its virtual power plant globally
articles.summary: 1KOMMA5° Australia is currently rolling out the Heartbeat AI technology while its European parent company has announced a 1 GW shiftable load milestone with its virtual power plant globally.

articles.title: Free electricity coming: are Aussies aware of Solar Sharer?
articles.summary: Australia is world-leading in solar adoption, with one in 3 homes equipped with a rooftop solar system. Ongoing volatility in fuel and energy prices is prompting Australian households to rethink how they power their homes and vehicles, with solar emerging as a way to buffer against rising costs.

articles.title: Microgrid developer granted $3 million to build clean energy ‘island’ blueprint
articles.summary: The first project funded under Stream A of ARENA's regional microgrid program has been awarded $3 million toward an overall $15 million cost to build a 7 MWp solar and 5 MW / 10 MWh battery storage system to island a New South Wales' community's energy security in the event of transmission failure.

articles.title: Grok Ventures backs 5 GWh thermal energy storage project in US
articles.summary: Grok Ventures has provided project-level financing as the sole external investor on a US-based 5 GWh thermal energy storage system providing 50 MW of round-the-clock energy through an offtake agreement with biofuels giant, POET.

articles.title: Developers must become ‘a bit of a firefighter, sound expert, and light expert’: Addressing BESS safety and community opposition
articles.summary: At a recent industry event hosted by market research firm Wood Mackenzie, energy storage experts discussed their approaches to public engagement, especially around addressing fire and other safety concerns.

articles.title: US ROUNDUP: BESS project advancements from OCI Energy, MN8 Energy, GridStor, Grenergy
articles.summary: In this US news roundup, OCI Energy, MN8 Energy, GridStor, and Grenergy advance battery energy storage system (BESS) projects in Texas, California, Colorado, and Georgia.

articles.title: Invinity lands contract for 2.1GWh flow battery in Switzerland, share price soars 60%
articles.summary: Invinity Energy Systems has been enlisted to design and deliver the world's largest flow battery, for a data centre project in Switzerland currently under construction by Flexbase.

articles.title: China’s HyperStrong deepens ties with European inverter manufacturer SMA for global utility-scale BESS projects
articles.summary: China-headquartered battery storage system integrator HyperStrong and German power electronics manufacturer SMA Solar have signed a strategic cooperation agreement.

articles.title: ASEAN regulatory frameworks ‘must evolve’ to capture full value of battery storage
articles.summary: “Policy and market frameworks must evolve” in ASEAN countries to unlock the full value of energy storage in the region’s energy transition, according to a new report.

articles.title: JA Solar appoints head of energy storage subsidiary, effective immediately
articles.summary: On May 20, JA Solar announced the appointment of Wang Junsheng as president of its energy storage subsidiary, with the appointment taking immediate effect.

articles.title: ‘More complex trading strategies’ necessary for BESS to be profitable in the UK
articles.summary: The UK’s NESO has cleared 150GW of BESS from its grid connection queues, according to figures from BloombergNEF.

articles.title: Equis launches GreenPoint Energy with 2.5GW of BESS and wind projects across Australia’s NEM
articles.summary: Singapore-based Equis has launched GreenPoint Energy, a wholly owned subsidiary consolidating its Australian renewable energy and battery storage operations under a dedicated platform with a 2.5GW portfolio of 12 battery energy storage systems (BESS) and wind projects across every National Energy Market (NEM)-connected state.

articles.title: Edify Energy to proceed with 2,400MWh solar-plus-storage sites in Australia
articles.summary: Edify Energy has reached financial close on two solar-plus-storage projects in Queensland, totalling 600MW/2,400MWh of BESS.

articles.title: EDP wins AU$3 million ARENA funding for bushfire-resilient microgrid in regional Australia
articles.summary: EDP Renewables Australia has secured AU$3 million in funding from ARENA to develop the Braidwood Renewable Microgrid Project.

articles.title: Researchers develop perovskite solar cells about 50 times thinner than conventional devices
articles.summary: Researchers at NTU Singapore have developed ultra-thin perovskite solar cells about 50 times thinner than conventional designs using a vacuum-based thermal evaporation process.

articles.title: Diagno secures Formbay backing for solar and battery diagnostics platform
articles.summary: Sydney-based startup Diagno has secured the support of Formbay to help scale up the rollout of its cloud-based platform used to monitor, diagnose, and optimise solar and battery energy storage systems.

articles.title: Enervest snaps up 300 MW battery as part of strategy shift
articles.summary: Australian energy developer Enervest has added a 300 MW battery project in northern New South Wales to its portfolio in a move that signals a strategy shift for the company.

articles.title: Edify reaches financial close on twin solar and battery projects
articles.summary: Edify Energy says it has reached financial close on its neighbouring Smoky Creek and Guthrie’s Gap projects in Queensland, the largest solar and battery hybrid projects under construction in Australia.

articles.title: Very low cost rooftop solar and batteries slashes domestic energy cost
articles.summary: Rooftop solar in Australia provides the cheapest, cleanest and most reliable domestic energy in history.

articles.title: OX2 starts building solar and battery project next to old coal mine
articles.summary: Swedish renewables developer OX2 has begun building a 135 MW solar farm and a 100 MW battery energy storage system at the site of an old coal mine in the New South Wales Hunter Valley.

articles.title: Sunraycer closes US$901 million financing for three Texas solar-plus-BESS projects
articles.summary: Independent power producer (IPP) Sunraycer Renewables has closed a US$901 million project financing facility, supporting three solar-plus-storage projects in Texas, US.

articles.title: Second-life BESS maker Moment Energy claims ‘world first’ safety certification as FEOC compliance crunch looms
articles.summary: Energy-Storage.news speaks with Moment Energy’s CTO and co-founder Gabriel Soares, and VP of business development, Carl Mansfield, about recent updates from the second life battery company.

articles.title: HyperStrong CEO on sodium-ion BESS product potential and challenges
articles.summary: Sodium-ion may be increasingly competitive for projects where total lifecycle economics matter more than initial procurement cost alone, the CEO of HyperStrong told us after the system integrator signed a 60GWh supply and co-operation agreement with CATL.

articles.title: US startup Antora deploys 5GWh thermal energy storage system at biofuels facility in South Dakota
articles.summary: California, US-headquartered thermal energy storage (TES) technology provider Antora has installed one of the world’s largest energy storage systems, in South Dakota.

articles.title: Changes in Hungary’s renewable energy subsidy regime, market evolution drive BESS investment opportunities
articles.summary: Hungary is entering a new phase of its energy transition, opening up new opportunities for energy storage investment. But bankability is still a challenge, so equity investment is needed, writes lawyer Daniel Orosz.

articles.title: Hitachi inks 20-year LTSA with Akaysha Energy for 298MWh battery storage system in Australia
articles.summary: Hitachi Energy has entered a 20-year service agreement with Australian developer Akaysha Energy to support the 155MW/298MWh Ulinda Park battery energy storage system (BESS) in Queensland's Western Downs region.

articles.title: Australia’s New South Wales seeks 2.5GW of generation and 12GWh of energy storage in state’s largest-ever tender
articles.summary: Australia's New South Wales (NSW) has opened two tenders seeking 2.5GW of renewable energy generation and 12GWh of long-duration energy storage, marking the largest generation procurement in the state's history under its Electricity Infrastructure Roadmap.

articles.title: NSW opens tenders for 2.5 GW of solar and wind alongside 12.5 GWh of storage
articles.summary: New South Wales is rolling out two of the biggest renewable energy tenders in the state’s history, seeking 2.5 GW of new generation capacity in addition to 12.5 GWh of long-duration energy storage.

articles.title: Flow Power adds three solar and battery projects to portfolio
articles.summary: Renewables developer and electricity retailer Flow Power has added almost 20 MW of new solar and 45 MWh of battery energy storage to its operational portfolio with the commissioning of three projects in South Australia.

articles.title: Glass frit chemistry explains EVA-driven degradation mechanisms in TOPCon solar cells
articles.summary: Researchers from University of New South Wales and Jolywood found that corrosion-induced degradation in TOPCon solar cells is primarily governed by glass frit chemistry in low-aluminum silver metallization. Their findings show that barium-zinc-modified frits significantly improve resistance to acetic-acid and damp-heat stress, enabling more stable silver-silicon interfaces and reduced module-level power loss.

articles.title: No country for old peakers: Price setting in the age of batteries
articles.summary: The dynamics that dictate how the price of electricity is set in Australia have been transformed in the last year, not by policy, but by the unusual characteristics of batteries. Unlike any generator before it, batteries are capable of playing both sides of the price-setting equation: supply and demand. Newly compiled data reveals the extent to which the National Electricity Market’s price-setting agenda has been utterly reconfigured.

articles.title: Lenders now actively financing merchant BESS projects in Germany
articles.summary: Lenders are increasingly willing to back large-scale BESS projects in Germany that don't have any contracted revenues, although that merchant risk is priced in to structures, an executive at Aquila Clean Energy EMEA said.

articles.title: EU Project Roundup: Neoen, Nofar, Enka, Return, RWE, Repono and others progress 3.3GWh of BESS capacity
articles.summary: Large-scale BESS projects have been progressed in Italy, Romania, Germany, Poland, Denmark and elsewhere in Europe, totalling around 1GW under construction, 1GWh soon to be, and another 1GWh at earlier stages.

articles.title: Ford Energy makes 20GWh, five-year North America supply deal with EDF
articles.summary: Ford Energy, the wholly owned subsidiary of Ford Motor Company and developer EDF power solutions North America have announced a five-year battery energy storage system (BESS) supply agreement.

articles.title: AC and DC augmentation in BESS: the differences between the two approaches
articles.summary: What are the different types of AC and DC augmentation in battery storage, and the pros and cons of each?

articles.title: Inovance’s Hong Kong IPO: storage fuels growth, targeting global top three PCS ranking
articles.summary: Chinese industrial automation and power technology firm Inovance Technology has filed its prospectus with the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, planning to raise around US$2 billion (RMB 13.7 billion) following strong growth in its energy storage business.

articles.title: Japan’s grid operators select 1.25GW of BESS in capacity market auction with 6-hour duration requirement
articles.summary: The Japanese association of grid operators has selected 19 battery storage projects totalling 1,251MW in the third running of a national capacity market auction.

articles.title: Malaysian utility TNB connects 100MW/400MWh grid-forming battery storage project to grid
articles.summary: Malaysian multinational utility company Tenaga Nasional Berhad (TNB) has launched a 100MW/400MWh battery storage project with an opening ceremony.

articles.title: Yarra Energy Foundation brings community battery paired with EV chargers online in Australia
articles.summary: Yarra Energy Foundation (YEF), a not-for-profit organisation, has commissioned a community battery storage system and two wheelchair-accessible electric vehicle (EV) charging bays in Clifton Hill, an inner-city suburb of Melbourne, Australia.

articles.title: Construction begins on OX2’s 300MWh Muswellbrook solar-plus-BESS in NSW, Australia
articles.summary: Swedish independent power producer (IPP) OX2 has confirmed it has started construction activities at the 100MW/300MWh Muswellbrook solar-plus-storage site in New South Wales (NSW), Australia.

articles.title: Australia’s Enervest shifts to own-and-operate model with 300MW battery storage system acquisition
articles.summary: Australian energy storage developer Enervest has acquired the Northern Border Battery, a 300MW battery energy storage system in development, from an unnamed Sydney-based developer.

articles.title: Bowen marks 400,000 home battery installation milestone
articles.summary: Australia is in the grip of a consumer-led energy storage boom with new figures showing more than 400,000 batteries representing 11.2 GWh of storage capacity have been installed nationwide in the past 10 months.

articles.title: GCL Technology expands beyond PV with new energy storage business
articles.summary: Solar module manufacturer and polysilicon producer GCL Technology is expanding into lithium-ion battery storage based on LFP materials as part of a shift toward a “multi-product” new energy platform.

articles.title: Non-lithium players Eos and ESS lean on future of US LDES deployment in Q1 2026 financials
articles.summary: Two battery energy storage system (BESS) companies, Eos Energy and ESS Tech Inc, are betting big on the US adoption of long-duration energy storage (LDES) in Q1 2026 financial reports.

articles.title: Canadian Solar plans to double battery cell and BESS manufacturing capacity, new CEO says
articles.summary: Canadian Solar intends to double its battery energy storage system (BESS) division’s manufacturing capacity, the company’s new CEO has said.

articles.title: Australia hits 400,000 home battery installations with 11.2GWh capacity in under a year
articles.summary: Australia has surpassed 400,000 home battery storage installations under the federal government's Cheaper Home Batteries Program.

articles.title: Zelestra energises 1GWh hybrid BESS project in Chile
articles.summary: Spanish developer and independent power producer (IPP) Zelestra has energised its 1GWh battery energy storage system (BESS) in the Atacama Desert in Chile.

articles.title: Rept Battero opens Indonesian cell and BESS manufacturing facility
articles.summary: China-based Rept Battero has officially opened its lithium-ion cell and BESS manufacturing facility in Indonesia.

articles.title: System-level BESS manufacturing defects cause ‘hidden risk’ for developers, Intertek CEA says
articles.summary: The “vast majority” of issues identified in Intertek CEA’s 2025 audits were system-level defects that could pose risks to projects if undetected and unresolved.

articles.title: EnerVenue piloting ‘30,000-cycle’ nickel-hydrogen BESS in China
articles.summary: Nickel-hydrogen battery company EnerVenue has announced a pilot battery energy storage system (BESS) project in Jintan, Changzhou, China.

articles.title: Iberdrola Australia submits 1GW battery storage system to the EPBC Act
articles.summary: Iberdrola Australia has submitted a 1,000MW battery energy storage system to Australia's EPBC Act for federal environmental assessment.

articles.title: Australia: NSW firming tender secures 2,128MWh of energy storage to address summer shortfall
articles.summary: Australia’s New South Wales has seen 532MW of firming projects contracted through its seventh infrastructure tender, securing two projects designed to strengthen electricity supply during periods of peak demand and system stress in the Sydney-Newcastle-Wollongong region.

articles.title: China’s Gotion and Richardson Electronics partner for US-made BESS
articles.summary: China-headquartered energy storage firm Gotion and US power electronics manufacturer Richardson Electronics have partnered to manufacture BESS. It comes at a time when Chinese companies are starting to sell down stakes in US assets amid new FEOC rules.

articles.title: Pew’s US DER policy playbook aimed at giving ‘every state a path forward’
articles.summary: Energy-Storage.news Premium speaks with Pat Wood III, co-chair at Pew Charitable Trusts about the company’s DER Policy Playbook

articles.title: Hydrostor to enter A-CAES project into Ontario LDES procurement
articles.summary: Long-duration energy storage (LDES) developer-operator Hydrostor intends to enter its compressed air storage project into Ontario, Canada’s recently announced LDES procurement.

articles.title: Nearly 13GWh of grid-scale BESS deployed globally in April
articles.summary: A total of 4.5GW/12.8GWh of large-scale BESS capacity when into operation around the world last month, with Asia and South America showing strong figures.

articles.title: INDIA Roundup: ACME Solar, IndiGrid commission Rajasthan & Gujarat BESS projects, GE Vernova wins pumped hydro contract
articles.summary: Battery storage and pumped hydro projects in India, in this edition of news in brief from around the world in energy storage.

articles.title: Akaysha Energy bags EPBC Act approval for 1,600MWh battery storage system in Victoria, Australia
articles.summary: Developer Akaysha Energy has received federal environmental clearance for its 400MW/1,600MWh Glenrowan battery energy storage system (BESS) in Victoria, with the project deemed "not a controlled action" under Australia's Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation (EPBC) Act.

articles.title: Battery Breakthrough Initiative hit as Australia cuts AU$1.3 billion in clean energy funding
articles.summary: Australia has clawed back AU$1.3 billion (US$940 million) in uncommitted clean energy manufacturing funding, with the Solar Sunshot Program and Battery Breakthrough Initiative among the programmes hit by budget cuts announced this week.

articles.title: Galileo CEO on ‘completely different’ development market, meeting PPA demand and where in Europe it will transact next
articles.summary: We catch up with Ingmar Wilhelm, co-founder and CEO of developer and IPP Galileo, after the firm commissioned its first own-operate renewable energy project.

articles.title: US ROUNDUP: BESS project updates from Spearmint, Polaris, and Clearway in Texas, Puerto Rico and Utah
articles.summary: In this US news roundup, battery energy storage system (BESS) project updates from Spearmint Energy in Texas, Polaris Renewable Energy in Puerto Rico, and Clearway Energy Group in Utah.

articles.title: Small solar and battery specialist adds another 27 projects as it buys up competitor
articles.summary:
Sydney-based "distributed" renewables outfit inks deal to buy portfolio of 27 small solar and battery projects, to bolster its supply of "low‑cost and reliable" energy.
The post Small solar and battery specialist adds another 27 projects as it buys up competitor appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Stunning growth of battery storage puts it at centre of global energy security needs, for cars and for grids

articles.title: Huge wind, solar and battery renewables project joins queue for federal green tick
articles.summary:
A massive hybrid renewables and battery project has joined the queue for federal environmental assessment, with state approvals already in hand.

articles.title: Snowy elbows out Origin and others to land $1.9 billion renewable power deal for state’s trains and electric buses
articles.summary:
Updated: Snowy Hydro lands $1.9 billion deal to supply 100 pct renewable power for state's train, tram and electric buses, beating out others after a two-year tender process.
The post Snowy elbows out Origin and others to land $1.9 billion renewable power deal for state’s trains and electric buses appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: Energy Insiders Podcast: Batteries take centre stage as world wrestles with fuel crisis
articles.summary:
BMI's Iola Hughes discusses the extraordinary growth of battery storage, for EVs and the grid, their key role in energy security, and the different chemistries. Plus: Fortescue's fast-tracks path to real zero, finally some big wind, and will Albanese seize the moment?
The post Energy Insiders Podcast: Batteries take centre stage as world wrestles with fuel crisis appeared first on Renew Economy.

articles.title: State threatens to step in if regulator and networks fail to remove EV charging “handbrakes” and sort out tariffs
articles.summary:
State says energy regulator and network companies must guarantee ready access to smarter and cheaper public electric vehicle charging, or face policy intervention.

articles.title: Transport for NSW inks $1.9 billion clean power deal with Snowy Energy
articles.summary: The New South Wales goverment has inked a seven year, $1.9 billion contract with federal government-owned Snowy Hydro retail arm Snowy Energy to power all public transport operations with renewable energy.

articles.title: Allume marks SolShare milestone connecting solar to 10,000 apartments
articles.summary: Victoria-based cleantech company Allume Energy has reached the significant milestone of connecting 10,000 apartments to solar energy in Australia, the UK, USA and Germany.

articles.title: Pacific nations call for 100% renewables, back fossil fuel non-proliferation treaty
articles.summary: On the back of state of emergencies recently declared across multiple Pacific nations, six island countries have united in a call for a fossil free Oceania and immediate transition to 100% renewable energy economies.

articles.title: Australia’s home energy success could be a global model in the midst of the energy crisis
articles.summary: With a volatile global fuel and energy crisis continuing relentlessly, Australia holds a quiet but powerful advantage that provides cost-of-living energy relief to millions by allowing our cars and homes to run on sunshine instead of fossil fuels.

articles.title: VIDEO – Energy Storage Summit 2026: Lessons learned from BESS construction projects
articles.summary: Building BESS is complicated, with myriad considerations around supplier contracts, site acceptance tests, data analysis, interface between different contractors.

articles.title: Sigenergy lists on Hong Kong Stock Exchange, raises HK$4.4 billion
articles.summary: On 16 April, battery storage solutions provider Sigenergy was listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange (HKEX) under the stock code 06656.HK.

articles.title: Hithium signs €400 million Spain battery gigafactory investment agreement after two-year courtship
articles.summary: Hithium has signed a formal investment commitment to establish a manufacturing facility in Spain's Navarre region.

articles.title: Akaysha inks deal with Copenhagen Energy for ‘mega-scale’ battery storage projects in Germany
articles.summary: Akaysha Energy has entered into a deal with Copenhagen Energy to advance “mega-scale” battery energy storage projects in Germany.

articles.title: Synergy submits 1GW Tathra Wind Farm with 500MW BESS to Australia’s EPBC Act
articles.summary: Synergy has submitted a hybrid renewable energy project to Australia's EPBC Act, featuring a 500MW battery energy storage system (BESS).
